nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=128 beats='Nia missed the last bus and got shut in overnight in the Windvale Museum of Clockwork Animals, a wonderland of brass and tin and copper creatures that have all wound down and gone perfectly still; her tiny wind-up mouse Tock rides on her shoulder, ticking softly as if to say come on, come on; four great wings open off the dark entrance hall, the brass aviary, the tin savanna, the copper deep-sea hall, and the crystal insect conservatory, and Nia must pick one to explore before her candle stub burns down'>>

choices.label: Slip into the copper deep-sea hall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Nia steps into the brass aviary where hundreds of clockwork birds sit frozen on their perches, beaks open in songs that stopped hours ago, and Tock ticks softly on her shoulder as if urging her to hurry'>>

choices.label: Walk in under the silent birds.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the aviary splits two ways, a wide floor of tall perch-trees leading deeper among the biggest birds, and a narrow spiral stair climbing to the high nesting boxes near the dark glass roof'>>

choices.label: Climb the spiral stair to the nests.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='among the perch-trees Nia can turn toward the songbird alcove full of tiny larks and wrens, or toward the great raptor roost where a huge brass eagle spreads its still wings'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the songbird alcove is a wall of tiny brass larks and wrens, each no bigger than a thumb, their little keys wound down to nothing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Tock scampers to a dusty music-box set into the wall, and Nia sees it once played all the birds at once, if only it had power'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='behind the music-box a small brass card is pinned, and it speaks of a master key that wakes the whole museum from the central rotunda'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Nia pockets the clue and follows Tock along a keeper walkway that runs behind the cases toward the middle of the building'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the walkway ends at a low service door marked with a picture of a great clock, the heart of the museum humming faintly beyond'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the raptor roost is all shadows and great still wings, a brass eagle and a bronze owl watching with glassy eyes that do not blink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a single long feather has fallen, shaped almost like a key, but when Nia lifts it she finds it is only a pretty decoy, not the real thing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the bronze owl, though, has one wing pointing stiffly toward an archway, as if a keeper set it that way long ago on purpose'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Nia follows where the owl points, down a short keeper stair toward the warm hum at the center of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways bring Nia to the aviary door that opens on the central rotunda, and Tock squeaks and darts ahead through the gap'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the door swings on a round hall lit by a high skylight of grey pre-dawn, the true middle of the whole clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='in the center of the rotunda stands the great museum clock, and Nia is sure the keys that wake everything must hang near it'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the spiral stair to the high nests is rickety, and halfway up one thin metal rung gives a warning crack beneath her shoe'>>

choices.label: Grab the rail and climb back down.
Climb on toward the high nests.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Nia freezes, grabs the rail with both hands, and waits until the shaking stops, her heart pounding, then backs carefully down to the safe aviary floor'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='safe on the floor again, she lets out a long breath, decides the high nests are not worth the danger, and turns instead to the perch-trees'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Nia climbs on despite the groaning, but the last rung folds and the stair pulls away from the wall, leaving her stuck on a narrow ledge among the sleeping birds; she is quite safe, but she can only wait up there, waving to Tock below, until the museum staff arrive at opening time and fetch a ladder to bring her down'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the tin savanna opens wide and golden, a whole plain of tin grass with a lion caught mid-roar, giraffes as tall as the ceiling, and a great elephant standing dark and heavy in the middle distance'>>

choices.label: Step out onto the tin plain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Nia can cross the open savanna floor toward the big animals, or slip along the painted horizon wall where a narrow walkway skirts the edge of the hall'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='out on the floor Nia can head for the elephant herd around the tin watering hole, or the giraffe grove where the tall necks reach up into shadow'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='up close the great tin elephant is enormous, and as Nia passes, a last bit of wind makes its trunk twitch and one foot scrape, and she jumps back with a gasp'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the elephant lurches a slow half-step, groaning, not truly awake but not truly asleep, and Nia sees she must give it a wide and careful berth'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she ducks behind a tin baobab tree and waits until the great beast settles again with a long mechanical sigh, still at last'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='safe past the elephant, Nia finds a worn keeper trail in the tin grass, little oil-drips leading toward the heart of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the trail ends at a plain door in the savanna wall, warm to the touch, with the hum of the great clock behind it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the giraffe grove is a forest of long tin necks and gentle glass eyes, their heads lost up in the roof shadows'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='high on a shelf, almost out of reach, Nia spies a ring shaped like a keyring, and a rope pulley the keeper used to raise and lower it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she works the pulley and lowers the shelf, but the ring holds only spare oil-cans, not keys, though a painted arrow points the way onward'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='following the arrow, Nia crosses the grove to a keeper door humming with the warmth of the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both routes meet at the savanna arch that opens on the central rotunda, and Tock slips under it with a flick of its tail'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='beyond the arch the round rotunda waits under its grey skylight, the true middle of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the great museum clock towers in the center, and Nia feels sure the keys that wake everything must hang somewhere near it'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the horizon-wall walkway is narrow, and as Nia edges along it the half-wound elephant lurches awake below and swings its heavy trunk, groaning, right across the way ahead'>>

choices.label: Press into an alcove and wait.
Try to hurry past the elephant.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Nia presses flat into a shallow alcove in the wall and holds still, letting the great elephant sway and settle until it goes quiet, then she slips back to the open floor'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='back on the savanna floor with the elephant still again, Nia decides the wall walkway is too risky and picks a path among the big animals instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Nia tries to hurry past on the narrow walkway, but the lurching elephant swings round and gently, blindly, backs her into the corner of the hall where she cannot get by; there is no harm in it, only a big slow tin beast in the way, and she must wait tucked safely in the corner until morning, when the keepers wind the elephant down and free her'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the copper deep-sea hall is cool and blue, great copper whales hanging on wires overhead, a huge octopus coiled in a corner, and glass tanks of still copper fish glinting in the dim light'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Nia can wade between the low floor tanks toward the middle of the hall, or climb the whale-rib catwalk that arches high over everything in the dark'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='among the tanks Nia can explore the coral cabinet full of little seahorses and fish, or the deep trench diorama where the water-glass turns almost black'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the coral cabinet glitters with copper seahorses and darting fish, all frozen, and a brass valve on its side hums when Nia rests her hand there'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Tock taps the valve and a panel slides back to show a folded chart of the whole museum, every hall drawn in careful ink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='on the chart a small tag is tied, shaped like a key, and a note in neat writing points to the rotunda at the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Nia tucks the chart away and follows its inked corridors, Tock leading, toward the warm middle of the building'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the way ends at a round copper door like a porthole, humming softly, the great clock ticking somewhere close beyond it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the deep trench diorama is the darkest place Nia has been, black glass all around, and only a faint glow far down in it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the glow comes from an anglerfish lamp with a little wind left, casting just enough light to show a low hatch set in the trench wall'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the anglerfish light flickers, and by it Nia reads a keeper sign above the hatch, an arrow and the painted shape of a clock'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she opens the hatch and follows a snug copper passage leading inward, warm and humming, toward the center of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways bring Nia to the round door of the deep-sea hall that opens on the central rotunda, and Tock squeezes eagerly through'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='past the door the great rotunda opens under a grey skylight, the true heart of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the huge museum clock stands at its center, and Nia is certain the keys that wake the animals must be near it'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the whale-rib catwalk sways as Nia climbs it, and out over the middle of the dark hall a copper rib swings loose with a clang, leaving a gap she would have to jump'>>

choices.label: Cling to the rope and back off.
Try to jump the gap in the dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Nia backs off the loose rib, clinging to the guide rope, and edges her way carefully back to the solid start of the catwalk, her hands aching'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='safe at the foot of the catwalk again, Nia decides the high dark crossing is not worth it and wades in among the floor tanks instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Nia tries to jump the gap in the dark, but she cannot see the far side and freezes on the swinging rib, too scared to go on or back; she clings there safely above the soft tank-nets until, at opening time, a keeper hears Tock squeaking and helps her down, her night of exploring over'>>

ending.title: Lost in the Dark Deep Hall

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the crystal insect conservatory glows softly, clockwork butterflies and beetles resting on glass ferns beneath a domed ceiling painted with stars, everything still and glittering'>>

choices.label: Wander in among the glass ferns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Nia can wander through the low fern beds among the resting insects, or climb the iron mezzanine gallery that runs around the glass walls above'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='in the fern beds Nia can visit the butterfly vault where foil wings hang folded, or the beetle corner where little brass beetles crowd a keeper bench'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the butterfly vault is full of clockwork butterflies with wings of thin bright foil, folded and still, waiting for a wind that never came'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Tock noses a tiny winder left on a stand, and Nia sees each butterfly has a keyhole no bigger than a seed, far too small for any ordinary key'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a card beside the winder tells of one master key that turns the whole museum at once from the central rotunda, and Nia memorises the way'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she follows the painted path out of the vault, Tock scurrying ahead between the glass ferns toward the middle of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the path ends at a mirrored door edged with brass, humming gently, the great clock beating slow and deep beyond it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the beetle corner is a busy little world of brass beetles gathered round a keeper bench scattered with tiny tools'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='in a half-open drawer Nia finds a folded map of the conservatory and the halls beyond, marked in a keeper hand'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the map shows a short way to the rotunda, and a small drawn beetle seems to point its feelers along the route'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Nia follows the mapped way between the glass cases to a mirrored door, warm and humming, that leads toward the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways lead Nia to the conservatory arch that opens on the central rotunda, and Tock slips through the crack ahead of her'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the arch gives on the round rotunda under its grey dawn skylight, the true center of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the great museum clock rises in the middle, and Nia knows the keys that wake everything must hang close by'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the mezzanine gallery is edged with old glass panels, and as Nia leans to look down, one tall panel shifts loose with a grinding sound and tips slowly inward'>>

choices.label: Step back from the loose glass.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Nia snatches her hands back and steps away as the loose glass panel settles crookedly against the rail, then she retreats along the gallery to the safe stairs down'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='back on the conservatory floor, shaken, Nia decides the mezzanine is too fragile and explores the fern beds below instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='Nia edges past the loose panel, but another shifts behind her and a glass door swings shut across the gallery, closing her gently in a bright little dead end; nothing breaks and she is quite safe, but she must wait, tapping to Tock through the glass, until the keepers open the gallery in the morning'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='BOTTLENECK: every wing of the museum leads here to the central rotunda, and Nia finds what she came for; high on a brass hook beside the great clock hangs the caretaker master key, the one key that can wind the whole menagerie at once; she climbs the little keeper ladder and lifts it down, warm and heavy, while Tock cheers in tiny squeaks; three ways lead on from the rotunda, through the tall spring-room doors, along the gallery of old keeper portraits, or down the narrow back stairs into the dark'>>

choices.label: Push through the great spring-room doors.
Follow the gallery of keeper portraits.
Slip down the narrow back stairs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the tall doors open on a vast machine room where the whole museum clockwork lives, wheels and shafts reaching up into the dark'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia follows a gantry between the still gears, Tock pattering ahead, toward a great silent drum at the very center of it all'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the gantry ends above the biggest mechanism Nia has ever seen, the master works of the entire menagerie, waiting cold and quiet'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the portrait gallery is lined with paintings of old keepers, and their painted eyes seem to follow Nia toward a door at the far end'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the last portrait shows a kind old man with oily hands, and beneath it a brass plate reads keeper Fettle, tender of the works'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the door below his portrait opens on a walkway that looks down into the great machine room, the heart of the whole museum'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the back stairs are cramped and dark, and at the bottom a keeper turnstile bars the way, its lock stiff with age; Nia can try to squeeze carefully through the gap beside it, or shove hard against the turnstile itself'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia breathes in and slips sideways through the narrow gap, Tock riding her collar, into a low passage beyond the turnstile'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the passage climbs and opens onto a ledge above the great machine room, the master works of the museum spread out below'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='Nia shoves the old turnstile with all her weight, but it swings the wrong way and latches shut behind her, penning her in the little dark stairwell with no way back up; she is safe and Tock keeps her company, but she can only sit and wait on the steps until the morning staff come and unlatch the turnstile to let her out'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='BOTTLENECK: all three ways bring Nia out above the great spring-room, and here she learns why the whole menagerie fell asleep; the enormous mainspring that drives every animal in the museum has snapped clean across, its broken ends sprung apart, and nothing can be wound until it is mended; even the master key is useless against a broken heart of steel; Tock scratches worriedly at the floor, and Nia sees three ways to look for help, a keeper toolbox, a wall of repair manuals, and a scratched patch of floor where Tock keeps digging'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the keeper toolbox is a deep tin chest of spanners and oil, and on top lies a heavy tool Nia does not yet know the use of'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Tock burrows under the tools and turns up a card of instructions, a keeper drawing of how a broken spring is clamped and changed'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='card in hand, Nia follows a keeper sign from the toolbox toward a low door half-hidden behind the great machine'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the wall of manuals is stacked with worn keeper books, their spines labelled winding, oiling, and mending the great spring'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia pulls down the mending book and its pages fall open to a picture of a hidden workshop where every spare part is kept'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the book names a door behind the machine, and Nia hurries to find it, Tock scampering at her heels'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='where Tock scratches, a loose floor panel hides a crawlspace, but a heavy gear leans over it, only half-braced; Nia can ease the panel up slowly and gently, or yank it free in a hurry'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia lifts the panel a careful inch at a time, keeping clear of the leaning gear, and uncovers a low crawlspace running behind the machine'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='she crawls through with Tock and comes up beside a small hidden door, the very one the keeper must have used'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='Nia yanks the panel free, but the sudden jerk unbalances the half-braced gear and it topples with a great clang across the crawlspace, blocking it and pinning the hem of her coat; she wriggles safely out of her coat, unhurt, but the way is buried now and she must wait by the broken spring until the keepers arrive at dawn to shift the gear'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='BOTTLENECK: each way leads to the same low door, and behind it Nia finds the caretaker’s hidden workshop, snug and warm and full of wonders; here are spare springs coiled on pegs, oil and clamps and the special tool for changing a mainspring, and on the bench a letter in keeper Fettle’s handwriting explaining that he has been taken poorly and cannot come, with careful drawings of how to mend the great spring; now Nia has the master key, a spare mainspring, and the knowledge to use them; three things call to her, the repair manual open on the bench, the coiled spare spring on its peg, and Fettle’s own letter'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the repair manual lies open at the very page Nia needs, each step of changing the great spring drawn in patient keeper ink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia reads it twice until she is sure she understands, Tock following her finger down the page like a tiny student'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='confident now, she gathers the tool and the spare spring and heads back toward the great spring-room to begin'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the spare mainspring hangs coiled and gleaming on its peg, as long as Nia is tall, heavier than it looks when she lifts it down'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='she loads it onto the little keeper cart with the changing tool, Tock perched on top to keep watch'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='wheeling the cart carefully, Nia makes her way back toward the broken heart of the museum'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='Fettle’s letter mentions a stiff old shortcut lever that could speed the heavy spring back to the spring-room; Nia can carry the spring the long safe way in her own arms, or throw the rusted shortcut lever to send the cart quickly'>>

choices.label: Carry the spring the long safe way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia lifts the heavy spring in her own arms and carries it step by careful step, Tock cheering her on, back toward the great spring-room'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='breathing hard, she sets the spring down beside the broken one, everything she needs now gathered in one place'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='Nia throws the rusted shortcut lever and the old cart jerks away too fast, rattling off down a side track with the spare spring aboard and vanishing behind a locked shutter; nothing is broken and Nia is fine, but the spare spring is out of reach now, and she can only wait by the machine until the keepers open the shutter in the morning'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='BOTTLENECK: every path ends here at the great spring-room, where Nia stands with the master key in one hand, a shining spare mainspring at her feet, and keeper Fettle’s instructions clear in her head; through the tall windows the sky is turning the soft grey of dawn, and soon the doors will open and children will pour in hoping to see the animals move; there is time for one brave choice; Nia can try to change the great spring and rewind the whole menagerie, mend just one beloved creature she has come to love, follow keeper Fettle’s deepest secret, or simply keep the museum safe and let the quiet dawn come'>>

choices.label: Change the great spring and rewind them all.
Keep the museum safe until dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='changing the great mainspring is the hardest task of all, and how Nia does it matters; she can work slow and careful by the manual, work bold and fast to beat the dawn, or try a clever trick from Fettle’s notes to make it easier'>>

choices.label: Work slow and careful by the manual.
Work bold and fast to beat the dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='step by careful step Nia clamps the broken spring, eases the ends apart, and fits the shining spare exactly as the manual shows, Tock passing her each tool'>>

choices.label: Fit the master key to the shaft.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the new spring is in and the master key fits the great winding shaft; Nia can wind it fully now so the whole menagerie wakes for the morning crowd, or wind it gently and set it to stir only when keeper Fettle himself returns'>>

choices.label: Wind it fully and wake them all for dawn.
Set them to wake when Fettle returns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='racing the light, Nia forces the spring in fast; it is nearly seated but not quite, and she must choose, to stop and reseat it properly or wind it now and hope'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia makes herself slow down, backs the spring out, and seats it properly this time, and the great works take the master key with a deep satisfying click'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='following Fettle’s clever notes, Nia links the spare spring through a helper gear that takes half the strain, and the mend goes smoother than she dared hope'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='with the trick done, the great mainspring holds firm, and the master key turns the whole museum with a low waking hum'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='Nia winds the master key and all through the museum the menagerie stirs to life at once, brass birds bursting into song, the tin lion stretching, the copper whales turning slow on their wires, the great elephant lifting its trunk in a gentle trumpet; when the doors open at dawn the children gasp with joy to see every animal moving, and keeper Fettle, hearing the news, weeps with gladness that a stranger saved his whole life’s work; the very best of endings'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='Nia sets the great spring gently, so the animals will wake slow and easy the moment keeper Fettle turns the key himself; she leaves everything mended and ready, and when Fettle comes home at last he finds his menagerie whole and waiting, and knows a kind hand has been here; a warm and thoughtful ending'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='the reseated spring holds, and Nia winds the museum awake; most of the menagerie stirs beautifully, and though a few small far-off animals sleep on, the morning crowd still marvels at a hall of moving wonders, and Nia grins to have very nearly done it all; a proud ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='Nia winds the half-seated spring and it slips with a twang, springing loose so the mend must be done all over again; nothing is harmed, but there is no time now before the doors open, and the animals sleep on through the morning while Nia waits, a little sad, to explain it all to the keepers; a gentle setback'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='Nia clever mend holds strong, and the master key wakes the menagerie smoothly, animal after animal coming alive down the halls; the keepers later shake their heads in wonder at the neat helper gear, and say the child who did this has the makings of a fine keeper; a clever, happy ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Nia cannot mend the whole great spring in time, but with the master key and a little spare wind she could wake just one animal, and she thinks of the ones she has met tonight; the brass lark, the tin lion, the copper whale, the crystal butterfly, or the gentle half-wound elephant'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia hurries to the aviary and fits the little winder to the brass lark tiny keyhole, turning it with the greatest care'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the lark trembles, ruffles its brass feathers, and Tock watches in delight as the little bird gathers itself to sing'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the brass lark bursts into the sweetest clockwork song, filling the whole aviary with silver notes, and when the doors open the children crowd to hear the one bird singing its heart out in the hush; keeper Fettle says a museum with even one waking animal is a museum full of hope; a bright, joyful ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia crosses to the tin savanna and fits the key to the great lion, winding until she feels its spring grow tight and ready'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the lion shivers, its tin mane shaking, and slowly lifts its heavy head as the last of the wind takes hold'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the tin lion opens its jaws and gives a long rolling roar that echoes through every hall, and the sleeping museum seems to shiver with it; the morning children jump and then cheer, and Nia laughs, proud of her brave waking beast; a grand ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia returns to the deep-sea hall and reaches up to wind the greatest copper whale where it hangs on its wires'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the whale flukes stir, its great copper body swinging slow, and a deep hum builds inside it like a song from far under the sea'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the copper whale lets out a long, low, beautiful call that rolls through the blue hall, and turns gently on its wires as if swimming; when the children come they stand hushed beneath it, gazing up in wonder, and Nia feels she has given the museum its very heart back; a wondrous ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia slips into the crystal conservatory and fits the seed-small winder to one clockwork butterfly with the lightest touch'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the foil wings quiver, unfold, and catch the grey dawn light in a hundred colours as the little spring takes hold'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the crystal butterfly lifts from its glass fern and flutters in slow bright circles beneath the starry dome, and one by one Nia winds a few more until a little cloud of colour drifts through the conservatory; the children who come will chase them with shining eyes; a delicate, magical ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia goes to the gentle half-wound elephant that frightened her earlier and, no longer afraid, fits the master key to wind it true'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the great tin elephant steadies as the proper wind fills its spring, its lurching gone, its movements smooth and calm at last'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the elephant that lurched so scarily in the dark now sways gently and lifts its trunk in a soft, friendly trumpet, tame and calm and no longer a runaway; the morning children ride giggling in a circle around it, and Nia is proudest of all to have made the frightening thing gentle; a heart-warming ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='keeper Fettle’s letter hinted at a deeper secret hidden in the museum, and Nia burns to know it; she can read his private journal on the bench, follow where a little museum clockwork mouse keeps leading, or open the one locked cabinet in the workshop'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the journal is thick and worn, and Nia turns its pages by candle light, Tock curled warm against her arm'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='page by page she learns how Fettle built the whole menagerie himself, animal by animal, over a long and lonely lifetime of loving work'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the last entry tells of his dearest wish, that one day a child would come who cared enough to keep the animals alive after him'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='Nia closes the journal understanding everything now, that the menagerie was one old man’s life poured lovingly into brass and tin, and that his greatest hope was simply a child like her; she leaves him a note promising to come back, and when Fettle reads it he knows his life’s work is safe; a tender, meaningful ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='a second clockwork mouse, twin to Tock, keeps darting ahead and looking back, plainly wanting Nia to follow; she can chase after it into the dark, or coax it gently to come to her instead'>>

choices.label: Chase the mouse into the dark.
Coax the mouse gently to you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the little mouse leads Nia to a tiny door behind the skirting where a whole nest of clockwork mice sleeps, Fettle’s secret family of helpers'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the mouse leads Nia to Fettle’s secret, a hidden nest of little clockwork mice he made for company, and she winds them one by one until a dozen tiny helpers scurry out to tend the sleeping museum; keeper Fettle is overjoyed that someone found his small friends, and Tock now has a whole family; a sweet, surprising ending'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='the little mouse will not be caught and simply sits, whiskers twitching, keeping Nia company through the last of the night; she never learns quite where it wanted to lead her, but she is not alone, and dawn finds the two of them waiting quietly together for the keepers to arrive; a calm, gentle ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the one locked cabinet in the workshop is old and beautiful, and Nia is sure it holds Fettle’s greatest secret; she can try the tiny keys on the master ring one by one, or force the little lock with a workshop tool'>>

choices.label: Try the small keys one by one.
Force the lock with a tool.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='patiently Nia tries key after key until at last one turns with a soft click, and the cabinet door swings open'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='inside stands the very first clockwork animal Fettle ever made, a small worn brass rabbit, older than all the rest, with a note that it must always be kept safe'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='the cabinet opens on Fettle’s first creation, a little brass rabbit worn smooth with love, the seed of the whole menagerie; Nia winds it with the greatest care and the old rabbit hops slowly, faithfully, as it must have a lifetime ago; keeper Fettle can hardly believe his first friend lives again, and calls Nia the finest thing that ever happened to his museum; a precious, moving ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='Nia forces the little lock with a tool, but the delicate old catch snaps and the cabinet jams tight shut, refusing to open at all; nothing is harmed that a keeper cannot mend, but she never sees the secret inside, and must wait rather sheepishly to tell Fettle she was too impatient; a small setback'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='perhaps the bravest thing is simply to keep the sleeping museum safe and let the quiet dawn come; but even so Nia has choices, she can let the animals sleep and wait, wind just one favourite before the light, or wait by the door to meet whoever comes'>>

choices.label: Let them sleep and wait for morning.
Wait by the door to meet someone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Nia settles on the great spring-room floor with Tock in her lap, watching the grey light climb the tall windows over the sleeping animals'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='Nia keeps the whole menagerie safe through the night and lets the dawn come gently, the animals still and sleeping, everything exactly as the keeper left it; she did not fix the great spring, but nothing came to harm, and when the doors open she can tell the keepers all she learned; a safe, quiet, unfinished ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Nia thinks of all the animals and picks the one that touched her most, hurrying to it with the master key as the sky pales'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='she fits the key with care and gives it just enough wind to wake for the morning, saving the rest for the keepers'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the animal stirs to gentle life under her hands, and she steps back to watch, glad she chose it'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='Nia wakes one single favourite animal to greet the dawn while the rest sleep on, safe and ready; when the children come they find one wonderful moving creature among the still ones, a promise of all the wonders to come once keeper Fettle mends the rest, and Nia treasures the quiet magic of her one small waking; a lovely, hopeful ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='Nia waits by the great front door as the light grows, and she can watch for the museum keepers arriving, leave a helpful note pinned where they will see it, or tuck herself out of sight until she understands who is coming'>>

choices.label: Leave a helpful note for the morning.
Hide until you know who it is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Nia writes out everything on a keeper pad, the broken spring, the spare, the steps to mend it, and pins it where the morning staff cannot miss it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='she props the master key beside the note, ready for whoever comes, and waits nearby to explain if they need her'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='the note Nia leaves tells the morning keepers exactly how to mend the great spring, and with it they wake the whole menagerie by opening time; the children never know how close the museum came to a silent day, but Nia knows, and the grateful keepers say her clear thinking saved the morning; a clever, satisfying ending'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='Nia waits quietly and meets the first keeper through the door at dawn, and tells her the whole story of the broken spring and the long night; the keeper listens amazed, thanks her kindly, and sees her safely home, and though Nia did not mend the menagerie herself, she leaves knowing she helped; a calm, friendly ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='Nia hides as the door opens, but it is only the early cleaner, who does not know a child is there and locks the inner gate on the way through, shutting Nia in the hall a while longer; she is safe and it is soon sorted out, but her chance to explain the broken spring slips by, and the menagerie sleeps through the morning; a gentle setback'>>
